title: ca_location
text: ca_location Specifies the location of the trust store that contains hashed, trusted CA certificates. ca_location | ca_loc = TrustStoreDir Arguments TrustStoreDir —relative or full path to directory containing the trust store Use Set this option in the assignment file of the application that is verifying the other. Description On some Linux machines, there are standard trust stores in locations like /etc/ssl/certs , /var/lib/ca-certificates , /etc/pki/tls/certs , or some other location. You can use the ca_location option to specify this location. Alternatively, you can use the ca_certificate option to specify a bundle with CA certificates. Example: Server Verification from a Unix TLS Client Explain this code ; client.asn on Unix [NET_SETTINGS] CheckServer verify_server=yes, ca_location=/etc/ssl/certs Copy Related Links ca_certificate Specifies the trusted CA certificate used to sign the certificate that is being verified.
